name: BEARPARK NO 31

surface_od_ft.qualifier: 323.69 A.O.D. typed (feet; ≈ 98.66 m AOD). Pencil '416.36' written above and '92.81' below the typed value; pencil calc beside header 324 − 240 = 84 (rockhead OD). 'shaft' struck through, 'bore' left in the LEVEL AT WHICH ... COMMENCED line.

units: feet/inches, transcribed verbatim — NO fathoms column on this 1962 NCB log; fathoms slot recorded as 0. Convert ONLY downstream.

pencil margin: 'RH 84' (rockhead at ~84 ft AOD; 323.69 − 240 = 83.69)
first bedrock below rockhead
geological classification (typed): HUTTON SEAM (L)
seam worked out — logged as Old Workings (void, 2 ft 10 in)
pencil margin: 'HU - 66' (Hutton at ~66 ft AOD)
cross-check clean: 255-0 + 2-10 = 257-10
seatearth beneath the Hutton
geological classification (typed): Open Hole (drilling method) restated at this level
'post' = sandstone girdles within the shale

intervals.verbatim: Seat-earth mudstone, light grey, slickensided with silty bands and ironstone nodules
annotations: 'Cored from 380' typed above this row — coring commenced at 380 ft; everything above was open-hole/chippings

intervals.verbatim: Mudstone, light grey, silty, rooty with fine sandstone partings, soft and slickensided at base

Mudstone, grey, silty at top, shaly at base with plant fragments
Seat-earth mudstone, light grey, soft and slickensided
Sandstone, white, fine grained, thin wispy bedded
Mudstone, dark grey, shaly with thin ironstone bands and mussel fragments at base
annotations: thin ironstone bands; mussel fragments at base (faunal marker approaching Harvey)

intervals.verbatim: Shale, black with large mussels and occasional Ostracods
annotations: faunal band: large mussels + occasional Ostracods

intervals.verbatim: Mudstone, black, irony with abundant Ostracods at top and occasional plants at base

geological classification (typed): HARVEY SEAM (N) — one of the two target seams (Purpose: prove Harvey (N) and Tilley (P))
50% core recovery
sum slip: 414-7 + thickness 1-10 = 416-5, clerk wrote depth 416-6 (1 inch discrepancy)

intervals.verbatim: Seat-earth mudstone, dark grey, soft and slickensided
annotations: seatearth beneath the Harvey

intervals.verbatim: Seat-earth mudstone, dark grey, soft and slickensided with ironstone bands

Sandstone, grey, medium grained with multi-directional cross bedding
Seat-earth mudstone, dark grey, soft and slickensided

Mudstone, light grey, silty with abundant roots
Sandstone, white, fine grained, irregular cross bedding with occasional slump beds at base

Mudstone, dark grey, shaly, micaceous with occasional roots and sandstone bands and partings
Mudstone, black, silty, finely micaceous, shaly with plant debris on bedding planes
Sandstone, greyish white, very fine grained with occasional shaly micaceous partings

seam_marks.how: named in original: geological classification 'HUTTON SEAM (L)'; logged as Old Workings (worked out, void). Pencil marginalia 'HU - 66' (Hutton at ~66 ft AOD).
named in original: geological classification 'HARVEY SEAM (N)'; COAL - bright, 1 ft 10 in, 50% recovery. Target seam of the borehole.
named in original: geological classification 'TILLEY SEAM (P)'; COAL - bright upper leaf (0-7) over a seat-earth parting (0-7) over a lower coal leaf (0-1, interval 48). Target seam of the borehole.

notes: Bearpark Colliery borehole 'near Club Lane, Whitesmocks' (BGS ID 847857; NZ24SE65; B/H Regd No 55; NCB borehole No 31). Purpose: to prove the Harvey Seam (N) and Tilley Seam (P). Sinker: N.C.B. No. 1 Rig; commenced 7/8/62, completed 24/9/62. Exact site NZ 25706 43348 (differs slightly from the catalogue point 425703,543341). LOG IS FEET/INCHES ONLY — there is no fathoms column on this 1962 NCB form, so units_system 'ffi' is used with the fathoms slot set to 0 in every array (feet in slot 2, inches in slot 3). Drilling: open-hole to 380 ft, 'Cored from 380'' (typed above interval 20); coring continued to base. Surface datum typed 323.69 A.O.D. (feet, ~98.66 m); a later pencil hand wrote '416.36' above and '92.81' below it, plus a pencil calc 324-240=84 (rockhead OD) beside the header — all recorded verbatim, meaning uncertain; the operative 323.69 is confirmed by the 324-240=84 rockhead working. Pencil geologist marginalia in the classification column: 'BCl - 240' (drift base), 'RH 84' (rockhead OD), 'HU - 66' (Hutton OD). Page corner pencil numbers 110/111/112 (BGS registration folio). ONE clerk arithmetic slip: interval 32 (Harvey coal) depth written 416-6 but 414-7 + thickness 1-10 = 416-5 (1 inch); transcribed as written. HOPKINS BAND (interval 31) is a named marker band immediately above the Harvey, not a coal — kept as annotation, not a seam. Only intervals 12, 32, 46, 47, 48 carry a written thickness; all other rows give depth-from-surface only (blank thickness recorded [0,0,0]), and the depth column is strictly monotonic. Watermarks/registration stamps present but the type is fully legible throughout. Final depth 469 ft 0 in = 142.95 m matches the BGS index depth exactly.
